name: Search for Password Files using grep or find (Unix)
notes:
This rule triggers when a search is made for the string "password" using the grep or find commands.
This behaviour could indicate a malicious actor searching for files containing passwords on a Unix system.
conditions_text: when an event matches any of the following BB:DeviceDefinition: Operating System
when the event category for the event is one of the following Audit.Command Execution Attempt
when the event matches Command Arguments (custom) is not N/A
when the event matches LOWER("Command Arguments") MATCHES '.*?"(grep|find)".*?"password.*' AQL filter query
TD_list: APPLY Search for Password Files using grep or find (Unix) on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when an event matches any of the following BB:DeviceDefinition: Operating System
AND when the event category for the event is one of the following Audit.Command Execution Attempt
AND when the event matches Command Arguments (custom) is not N/A
AND when the event matches LOWER("Command Arguments") MATCHES '.*?"(grep|find)".*?"password.*' AQL filter query
RED_list: Event name: Search for Password Files using grep or find
Event description: A search for passord files using grep or find has been detected.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Machine Identifier (custom)
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
